title: Cat Food Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate daily cat food portions based on weight and activity. Get personalized feeding recommendations. Free, fast, and updated for 2026.
intro: Proper cat nutrition depends on weight, activity level, and life stage. This calculator determines your cat's daily food amount using the 20-30g per kilogram standard recognized by veterinary organizations worldwide. Quick, free, and updated for 2026.
keyTakeaway: **Daily Cat Food Formula**: Calculate your cat's daily feeding amount using weight and activity level. Standard: 20-30g per kilogram. Free reference for proper nutrition.
useCases: Pet owners determining proper daily feeding amounts
Quick reference for personalized feeding plans
explanation:
**Cat care** follows international veterinary guidelines (WSAVA, AAHA). Nutrition, vaccination, and parasite prevention have species-specific, breed-specific, and age-specific protocols.

## How to Calculate

Your cat's daily food portion depends on weight and activity level, using the 20-30g per kilogram standard. This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside normal parameters.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an informational guide. For critical decisions (health, medical, nutritional), consult with a licensed veterinarian. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much should I feed my cat daily?
faq.a: Most cats need 20-30 grams of food per kilogram of body weight daily. Adjust based on activity level, age, and health status. Consult your veterinarian for personalized recommendations.

faq.q: How do I calculate cat food portions by weight?
faq.a: Multiply your cat's weight in kilograms by 20-30g. A 5kg cat typically needs 100-150g of food daily. Activity level may increase or decrease this amount.

faq.q: Does activity level affect how much cats should eat?
faq.a: Yes. Active cats may need more calories, while indoor or less active cats may need less. Adjust portions accordingly, and monitor your cat's weight.

faq.q: Is this calculation accurate for all cats?
faq.a: This is a general guideline using international standards. Individual cats vary by breed, metabolism, and health. Always consult your vet for specific needs.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free and don't require registration or login.

faq.q: Do you store my cat's information?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any data to external servers.

faq.q: How often should I adjust my cat's food portions?
faq.a: Review portions monthly and adjust if your cat's weight changes. Senior and overweight cats may need different amounts. Regular vet checkups help optimize nutrition.

faq.q: What's the best food for different cat ages?
faq.a: Kittens, adult cats, and seniors have different nutritional needs. Kittens need more protein, while seniors may need modified portions. Consult your veterinarian for age-appropriate feeding.
